symbols: ham on silver platter — a luxurious cut of cured meat presented as an object of aesthetic contemplation
table knife — the instrument of carving and consumption, the mundane made monumental
white tablecloth — domestic refinement and the painter's interest in rendering light on fabric
dark background — contrast that isolates the subject, elevating the ordinary to the status of art
description: A close-up still life depicting a large piece of ham with a dark, caramelized exterior and pink-golden interior, resting on a round silver platter atop a white tablecloth. A small table knife with a pale handle lies in the foreground. The background is dark brown with loosely painted, indistinct forms. The brushwork is broad and confident, with thick impasto rendering the glistening fat and meat. The artist's signature is visible at the lower right.
